name: Citrus Marinade and Salsa (for Chicken or Fish)
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Tomato, peeled, seeded and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. For the marinade, combine lemon juice, dry sherry, olive oil, dried oregano, and garlic salt in a 1-cup measure.
2. Set aside 2 tablespoons of the marinade for brushing during grilling.
3. Pour the remaining marinade into a plastic bag placed in a bowl. Add chicken or fish pieces to the bag, seal it tightly, and chill for at least 1 hour, turning occasionally.
4. For the salsa, combine the reserved 2 tablespoons of marinade with chopped tomato, sliced green onion, olives, parsley, and almonds. Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.
5. When ready to cook, drain chicken or fish from any remaining marinade, then grill until chicken is cooked through and fish flakes easily with a fork. Brush often with the reserved marinade for flavor.
6. Serve grilled chicken or fish with chilled salsa and avocado slices, if desired.
Nutrition facts per tablespoon of salsa: 13 calories, 1 gram total fat (0 grams saturated fat), 0 milligrams cholesterol, 13 milligrams sodium, 1 gram carbohydrates, 0 grams fiber, 0 grams protein. Daily Value: 1% Vitamin A, 5% Vitamin C, 0% Calcium, 0% Iron.
